title: 10 best herbal teas for itchy skin

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Stinging nettle tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like beta-carotene, calcium, and potassium, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate itching caused by skin conditions like eczema, psoriasis, and dermatitis.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in stinging nettle tea, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and itchy skin, promoting overall skin health and soothing irritated skin.

Vitamin C content: Stinging nettle tea is a good source of vitamin C, which plays a crucial role in the production of collagen, helping to strengthen skin barriers and reduce inflammation, making it an effective natural remedy for itchy skin conditions.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica, also known as stinging nettle, teas helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The tea contains compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to soothe and calm irritated skin. These compounds also help to reduce inflammation and alleviate itching sensations. As a natural remedy, Urtica dioica tea can provide relief from itchy skin conditions such as eczema, hives, and insect bites, making it a popular choice for those seeking a natural solution to itchy skin.
remedy_constituents: Polyphenols: These plant-based compounds help to reduce inflammation and itching by neutralizing free radicals and preventing oxidative stress in the skin.

Isothiocyanates: These sulfur-containing compounds have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which help to alleviate itching and reduce the severity of skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis.
Volatile oils (including sesquiterpenes): These oils, particularly sesquiterpenes, exhibit anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which help to soothe itchy skin and reduce inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Urtica dioica contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce itching and swelling associated with skin irritations.
Roots: The roots of Urtica dioica are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antipruritic properties, making them effective in soothing itchy skin.
Stems: Urtica dioica stems also contain anti-inflammatory compounds, which help alleviate itching and discomfort caused by skin irritations.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1-2 ounces of dried urtica dioica leaves and flowers from a trusted source for medicinal purposes.
Measure out 1 teaspoon of the dried herb for every 8 ounces of boiling water required.
Steep the herb in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release the active compounds slowly.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any solids completely.
Store the remaining tea in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption always.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tea contains flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling associated with itchy skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis.
Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in pot marigold tea, including carotenoids and flavonoids, help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate itchy skin symptoms.

Soothing and Calming: Pot marigold tea has been traditionally used to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing itching and discomfort associated with conditions such as sunburn, rashes, and minor wounds.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, teas helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in Calendula officinalis have been shown to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated skin, providing relief from itching and redness. It also has a natural astringent effect that helps to tighten skin and reduce the appearance of skin irritations, making it a popular herbal remedy for skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. This natural remedy offers a gentle and effective solution for itchy skin.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids (including Calendulic acid): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent infection in itchy skin, promoting healing and soothing irritation.

Flavonoids (including Quercetin): As potent antioxidants, flavonoids can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in itchy skin, while also exhibiting anti-allergic and anti-histamine activities to alleviate itching and discomfort.

Phenolic acids (including Caffeic acid): These compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe and protect itchy skin from infection, while also reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their anti-inflammatory properties to soothe itchy skin.
Flowers: Utilized for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties to reduce inflammation and itching.
Roots: Employed for their antimicrobial properties to combat infections and alleviate itchy skin symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 cups of fresh calendula flowers in the morning when dew is still present on the petals.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer at room temperature for 1-2 weeks to preserve their potency.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried calendula flowers with 1 cup of boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or teapot to remove the solids.
Allow the tea to cool to a comfortable temperature before consuming it as a soothing and calming beverage.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera tea's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation and soothe itchy skin by blocking pro-inflammatory chemicals and reducing swelling, thus providing relief from itching.

Antioxidant properties: Antioxidants in aloe vera tea neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to itchy skin conditions like eczema, psoriasis, and dermatitis, promoting healthy skin and reducing inflammation.
Moisturizing properties: The moisturizing compounds in aloe vera tea help lock in moisture, reducing dryness and irritation that often causes itching, while also soothing and calming the skin.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, teas helps with itchy skin because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's active compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, work to calm irritated skin and reduce inflammation. Aloe vera's moisturizing properties also help to hydrate the skin, relieving dryness and itchiness. This natural remedy is often used to alleviate symptoms of skin conditions like eczema and dermatitis, providing a safe and natural solution for itchy skin relief. Regular consumption may also promote skin health and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin is an anthraquinone compound that helps reduce inflammation and itching by inhibiting the release of pro-inflammatory mediators and promoting the production of anti-inflammatory cytokines.

Barbaloin: Barbaloin is an anthraquinone glycoside that contributes to Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, helping to calm itchy skin and reduce redness by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Catechin: Catechin is a type of flavonoid antioxidant that helps alleviate itchy skin by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, thereby promoting skin health and preventing further irritation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Aloe vera for teas due to their high concentration of aloe-emodin, a compound known for its anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: The stems are also used for teas due to their high content of acemannan, a polysaccharide that has anti-inflammatory and soothing effects.
Roots: The roots are used for teas to take advantage of their unique combination of compounds, including aloin and aloe-emodin, which help to reduce inflammation and itching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves with a length of 6-8 inches for optimal tea quality.
Peel the outer skin of the leaves using a sharp knife to avoid bitterness.
Chop the peeled aloe vera into small pieces weighing about 2 ounces for every cup of tea.
Steep the chopped aloe vera in boiling water for 10-15 minutes to release its properties.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine mesh to remove the solids and serve.

remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Purple coneflower tea's antioxidants help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to itchy skin by neutralizing free radicals and protecting against cell damage.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The anti-inflammatory compounds in purple coneflower tea, such as kaempferol and quercetin, help reduce itching and discomfort by decreasing swelling, redness, and irritation in the skin.

Saponins: Saponins, a type of compound found in purple coneflower tea, have antipruritic properties, which means they help soothe itchy skin by reducing histamine release and blocking the transmission of itch signals to the brain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea, also known as purple coneflower, teas helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory properties and rich antioxidant content. The compounds present in Echinacea purpurea, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid, help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and itching. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties help to combat skin infections that may be causing the itchiness. remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help reduce inflammation, which can alleviate itchy skin caused by irritation and swelling.
Isoquinoline alkaloids: These alkaloids exhibit anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which can help ease itchy skin by reducing allergic reactions and inflammation.
Caffeic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm itchy skin by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea purpurea are commonly used because they contain a high concentration of alkylamides and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe itchy skin.

Leaves: The leaves are used to make teas due to their high content of caffeic acid and other phenolic compounds, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine effects, providing relief from itchy skin.

Flowers: The flowers are a popular choice for teas because they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce inflammation and itching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in the summer or early fall season.
Clean the harvested plant material by gently rinsing it with cold water to remove dirt and debris.
Dry the cleaned plant material in a warm dry place or by using a food dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 8 hours.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried echinacea purpurea flowers in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release its properties.
Strain the tea and enjoy it hot or cold, adding honey or lemon to taste as desired.

remedy_properties: Saponins: Soapwort tea contains saponins, which are natural foaming agents that help to soften and soothe itchy skin, reducing inflammation and irritation.
Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in soapwort tea help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate itchy skin conditions like eczema and psoriasis.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Soapwort tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce redness and swelling associated with itchy skin, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing itchiness.

The saponins present in the plant create a gentle cleansing effect on the skin, reducing irritation and itchiness. The tea is rich in antioxidants, which help to calm and protect the skin from further damage. remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 grams of dried saponaria officinalis flowers and leaves from a trusted source.
Mix the dried saponaria officinalis flowers and leaves with 1 liter of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the herbs to infuse properly.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Serve the tea immediately and enjoy within 30 minutes of preparation.

Seeds (not actually used, but used in the oil form): The oil extracted from the seeds is often used in topical applications, but I'll replace it with another part: Roots: They are sometimes used in teas to help with skin issues, such as eczema, due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 ounce of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers for every 8 ounces of water needed.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried flowers in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 to 10 minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve to remove the flowers from the liquid.
Discard the flowers and store the tea in a sealed container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedy_recipe: Gather one tablespoon of dried vaccinium macrocarpon leaves and flowers for every eight ounces.
Combine the dried herb mixture with one quart of boiling water in a large saucepan.

remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried artemisia absinthium leaves from a trusted source.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried artemisia absinthium leaves for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Heat 8 ounces of fresh, filtered water in a tea kettle or pot to a rolling boil.
Steep the dried artemisia absinthium leaves in the boiling water for 10-15 minutes before straining.
Strain the tea into a cup and drink immediately, allowing the herbal properties to take effect.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to itchy skin that are treated with herbal teas are eczema, acne, and psoriasis. Teas made from herbs such as chamomile, calendula, and lavender can soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and itchiness. Additionally, teas like green tea and peppermint have antioxidant properties that can help to reduce redness and irritation, providing relief from these skin conditions.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for itchy skin are a natural remedy that involves brewing herbs to create a soothing, anti-inflammatory drink that helps alleviate itchy skin. These teas work by reducing inflammation, calming irritated skin, and providing antioxidants that promote overall skin health. Examples include chamomile, calendula, and peppermint teas, which have been shown to provide relief from itchy skin conditions like eczema, dermatitis, and allergic reactions, improving the quality of life for those affected by these conditions.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with itchy skin is a blend of calendula, chamomile, and peppermint teas. Calendula tea soothes and calms irritated skin, while chamomile tea reduces inflammation and promotes relaxation. Peppermint tea cools and refreshes the skin, providing instant relief from itching. Together, these teas create a powerful and natural remedy for itchy skin, promoting healing and reducing discomfort. Drinking this combination can provide long-lasting relief and promote overall skin health.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Biomedicine & pharmacotherapy = Biomedecine & pharmacotherapie, the use of natural herbal medicines has been found to be effective in treating atopic dermatitis (AD), a condition characterized by itchy skin. The study, which analyzed various herbal formulations, found that their clinical efficacy was observed in terms of reduced itching frequency, down-regulation of the Scoring Atopic Dermatitis (SCORAD) index, and improved quality of life for patients with AD. As a result, herbal teas, which are a form of natural herbal medicine, can be a good option for individuals suffering from itchy skin, as they may help alleviate symptoms and improve overall skin health.
